round: 1
date: 2026-04-26
target: stage-3/current-plan.md
task: quoin-foundation
class: A
---

## Verdict: REVISE

## Summary

The plan is structurally strong (matches stage-1 rigor; fixture-first ordering; deterministic tests; risk-register depth) but has three load-bearing defects: (a) two non-trivial orchestrator skills (`/run`, `/architect`) hardcode planning-artifact paths and are missing from the plan task that edits 8 SKILL.md files, so a `/run stage-3 of <task>` invocation would silently break the entire stage routing; (b) the plan's anchor lines for SKILL.md edits are imprecise in places (plan/SKILL.md anchor line 16 vs actual line 17; gate/SKILL.md line 116's prose contains a `<task-name>/` reference where `current-plan.md` lands AFTER the closing backtick, so the residual-hardcode grep regex won't match); (c) the `mixed/` fixture in the plan's first task does not represent the real production grandfathering case — `artifact-format-architecture/` has NO `architecture.md` at all (NOT an `architecture.md` without `## Stage decomposition` as the fixture stages), so the live-codebase test exercises a different code path than the plan's claimed "load-bearing R-NINE / I-FIVE assertion."

## Issues

### Critical (blocks implementation)

- **Issue C1 — `/run` and `/architect` SKILL.md hardcode artifact paths but are missing from the plan task that edits 8 SKILL.md files**
  - What: The plan task that edits SKILL.md files enumerates 8 targets. Two skills not in the list ALSO hardcode planning-artifact paths and would silently route to the wrong place after this stage merges:
    - `dev-workflow/skills/run/SKILL.md:132`: `Artifact: .workflow_artifacts/<task-name>/current-plan.md`
    - `dev-workflow/skills/run/SKILL.md:145`: passes path to `current-plan.md` to spawned `/implement`
    - `dev-workflow/skills/run/SKILL.md:192`: `Artifact: .workflow_artifacts/<task-name>/review-<N>.md`
    - `dev-workflow/skills/architect/SKILL.md:20`: `Read the task subfolder if it exists (prior architecture.md, current-plan.md)`
  - Where: plan task list (the 8-row table); architecture line 164 listed exactly these 8 files, but the architecture line was a sketch — the plan was supposed to verify completeness via cross-skill audit and didn't.
  - Why it matters: `/run` chains the entire workflow including `/implement`. If `/run stage-3 of quoin-foundation` is invoked, the orchestrator's SKILL.md still says `current-plan.md` lives at the task root. It would either pass the wrong path to its spawned `/implement` subagent, OR the subagent (which DID get edited) would resolve correctly while `/run`'s own status messages report the wrong path. Either way the user gets confused state. Lesson 2026-04-13 ("when changing how a skill resolves paths, the orchestrator skills referencing it by name must also be updated") applies directly. Lesson 2026-04-14 ("/critic should explicitly check all files that the newly-added skill references by name") is the canary that should have caught this at planning time.
  - Suggestion: Expand the SKILL-edit task's table from 8 to 10 rows. Add `run/SKILL.md` (3 anchors: line 132, 145, 192) and `architect/SKILL.md` (1 anchor: line 20). Confirm `architect/SKILL.md:303` (the architecture-critic-N.md write target — explicitly task root per the plan's D-THREE) is ALSO touched to add a comment that says "architecture-critic-N.md ALWAYS at task root regardless of stage." Update the references-to-architecture in the plan to note the 8-file architecture sketch was incomplete; flag the discrepancy in the Decisions section. The final residual-hardcode grep's lower bound rises from 8 to 10.

- **Issue C2 — `mixed/` fixture in the plan's first task does not match the real production case it claims to grandfather**
  - What: The plan states the `mixed/task-c/` fixture (the third sub-step of the fixture-author task) "represents `artifact-format-architecture` and `caveman-token-optimization` real-world shape." It encodes: `architecture.md` (NO `## Stage decomposition` heading) + root `current-plan.md` + `stage-1/current-plan.md`. But the actual production layouts at the time of this critic are:
    - `.workflow_artifacts/artifact-format-architecture/`: NO `architecture.md` at all + root `current-plan.md` + `stage-5/measurement-report.md` (NO `current-plan.md` inside `stage-5/`).
    - `.workflow_artifacts/caveman-token-optimization/`: HAS `architecture.md` WITHOUT `## Stage decomposition` (verified by grep) + root `current-plan.md` + NO `stage-N/` subfolders at all.
    - `.workflow_artifacts/v3-stage-3-smoke/` and `.workflow_artifacts/v3-stage-4-smoke/`: HAVE `architecture.md` WITH `## Stage decomposition` + root `current-plan.md` + NO `stage-N/` subfolders.
  - Where: the fixture-author task step c (the `mixed/` directory definition); the resolver-unit-test case named "load-bearing R-NINE / I-FIVE assertion"; the e2e smoke case that loops over the four real in-flight task folders.
  - Why it matters: The unit test is named "the load-bearing R-NINE / I-FIVE assertion" — but the fixture's exact shape (arch.md w/o decomp + root plan + stage-1 subfolder simultaneously) does NOT exist in production. The four real in-flight tasks each fall into different rule-3 sub-cases:
    - `artifact-format-architecture` → resolver hits rule 3 because `stage=None` AND no architecture.md; arch.md absence is `not arch.exists()`, not `arch.exists() and no decomp`.
    - `caveman-token-optimization` → resolver hits rule 3 because `stage=None`; arch.md exists but no decomp.
    - `v3-stage-3-smoke` and `v3-stage-4-smoke` → resolver hits rule 3 because `stage=None`; arch.md HAS decomp (stage=None still routes to root, but the test fixture for this exact shape is the `multi-stage/task-b/` case, NOT `mixed/`).
    Per lesson 2026-04-22 (fixture-first traps when baselines rot): the unit test "passes" because rule 3 routes everything `stage=None` to root regardless of whether decomp exists or arch.md exists or stage-1/ subfolder exists. The test does not actually discriminate between the three production shapes; it merely confirms rule 3's invariant. The "load-bearing R-NINE / I-FIVE assertion" claim is therefore overstated.
  - Suggestion: (a) Rename the `mixed/` fixture to `mixed-with-decomp-only/` and clarify in the rationale that it represents a SYNTHETIC worst-case (a future task that DID partially migrate to stage-1/ then reverted) — NOT a real production layout. (b) Add four explicit unit sub-cases naming each real layout: one for arch.md absent (covers `artifact-format-architecture`), one for arch.md present without decomp (covers `caveman-token-optimization`), one for arch.md present with decomp (covers `v3-stage-3-smoke` / `v3-stage-4-smoke`), and one for the synthetic `mixed/` case. (c) Modify the e2e case to assert against the EXACT four production folder layouts independently — currently the case loops over all four with the same assertion, which masks any shape-dependent routing bug. Add a per-folder rationale comment naming which rule-3 sub-case each folder exercises.

- **Issue C3 — Anchor-line precision is off in plan/SKILL.md and gate/SKILL.md, and `/critic` line 113 is a write-target reference, not an edit anchor**
  - What: Three anchor-line slips:
    - `plan/SKILL.md` (the plan's row 2 of the SKILL-edit table) says "session-bootstrap step 3 at line 16" but line 16 reads `2. Read .workflow_artifacts/memory/sessions/ for active session state` — the actual `Read the task subfolder` line is line 17, not 16.
    - `critic/SKILL.md` (row 3) lists FOUR anchors (lines 15, 33, 113, 122). Line 113 is `Write critic-response-{round}.md using the §5.4 Class A writer mechanism` — this is a section-heading line, not a `current-plan.md` or `critic-response-N.md` path reference, and the resolver-prose template does not fit cleanly there. Line 122 IS the actual write-target sentence (`{path} is {project-folder}/.workflow_artifacts/{task-name}/critic-response-{round}.md`). Also line 17 in critic/SKILL.md (`Append your session to the cost ledger: .workflow_artifacts/<task-name>/cost-ledger.md`) is NOT in the edit list — but per D-THREE cost-ledger stays at task root regardless, so this is correct (an exclusion, not an omission). Document the exclusion explicitly.
    - `gate/SKILL.md` line 116 reads (paraphrased): "The task subfolder for artifacts" followed by an inline code span containing `.workflow_artifacts/TASKNAME/` — then prose listing `architecture.md, current-plan.md, ...` AFTER the closing backtick. So the line contains a single `TASKNAME/` reference (literally written as the angle-bracketed placeholder) AND the literal substring `current-plan.md` — but they are NOT adjacent: between them sit a closing backtick, a colon, the word `architecture.md,` and a space. The plan's residual-hardcode grep regex `TASKNAME/current-plan` (literal angle-bracket placeholder + path) won't match because the regex expects adjacency. The line-116 edit needs to either rewrite the prose entirely to match the resolver pattern, OR the grep regex must be widened to also catch "task subfolder ... current-plan" prose.
  - Where: SKILL-edit task table; final residual-hardcode grep procedure.
  - Why it matters: `/implement` (the Sonnet implementer) is instructed to find these exact line numbers. An off-by-one anchor + an anchor pointing at a non-edit-fitting line leads to scattered edits or skipped edits. Per lesson 2026-04-23 (rubric refs belong at the write-site) and lesson 2026-04-13 (cross-skill SKILL.md edits): the implementer needs the EXACT edit boundaries, not just "line 16-ish." The gate/SKILL.md prose-form mismatch is a special concern: the residual grep is the canary for missed edits, and it would silently false-pass on gate/SKILL.md line 116 because the regex doesn't match the prose shape there.
  - Suggestion: (a) Verify each anchor line by reading 3 lines of context; rewrite anchors as `(line N–M, "exact opening words of the line ...")` form matching the existing stage-1 plan's anchor convention (see `.workflow_artifacts/quoin-foundation/finalized/stage-1/current-plan.md`'s anchor style). (b) Drop `critic/SKILL.md:113` from the anchor list (it's a section-heading line, not an edit site); keep line 122 as the single write-target anchor for that file. (c) Rewrite gate/SKILL.md line 116 to a form the residual grep can match — e.g., split the prose to put `<task-name>/current-plan.md` adjacent — OR widen the residual grep to additionally grep `'task subfolder for artifacts'` and assert the resolver-prose phrasing replaced it. (d) Add an explicit "cost-ledger.md path is excluded from the residual-hardcode sweep — it stays at task root per D-THREE" carve-out to the grep documentation, and verify all 8 (or 10 per C1) modified SKILL.md still reference cost-ledger.md at the task root.

### Major (significant gap, should address)

- **Issue M1 — Resolver API ambiguity: rule-2 silently raises on missing arch.md but rule-3 doesn't, and the SKILL.md call pattern doesn't expose this**
  - What: The resolver has asymmetric exception behavior:
    - `task_path("x", stage="name")` w/ no arch.md → raises `ValueError("architecture.md missing")`.
    - `task_path("x", stage=None)` w/ no arch.md → returns task root silently.
    - `task_path("x", stage=3)` w/ no arch.md → returns `task-x/stage-3/` silently (rule 1 is unconditional).
    The CLI mirrors this: name-lookup miss exits 2, others exit 0. But the SKILL.md call pattern (the plan's per-file edit template) reads: `task_dir = python3 ~/.claude/scripts/path_resolve.py --task <task-name> [--stage <N-or-name>]` — no error-handling guidance. If the orchestrator's parser passes `--stage <name>` and arch.md is missing, the SKILL.md prose tells the agent NOTHING about what to do with exit 2.
  - Where: resolver exception contract spec; per-file edit template; thorough_plan §1 rewrite.
  - Why it matters: When the resolver errors via rule 2, the cheapest path is to fall back to rule 3 (return task root) AND warn the user that a name was not resolved. Currently the SKILL.md prose just says "compute the path" without specifying what to do on failure. Lesson 2026-04-25 (`set -u` rc-hang) is the analog: shell-environment semantics need explicit prose about the fallback path; the same applies to script error semantics. Without this, an `/implement` agent that hits exit 2 will halt the orchestration with a generic Python traceback instead of falling back to root.
  - Suggestion: Add to the per-file edit template an explicit error-handling clause: "If `path_resolve.py` exits with code 2 (rule-2 ambiguous-stage failure), interpret as: stage name was not resolvable from architecture.md. Fall back to the task root (rule 3) AND ask the user to disambiguate using the integer form `stage <N>`. Do NOT abort the orchestration." OR change the resolver itself to fall back to rule 3 silently on rule-2 miss (with a stderr warning) — drop the exit-2 path entirely. The latter is simpler; the former is more explicit.

- **Issue M2 — The real-repo unit and e2e cases introduce a hidden git-state dependency**
  - What: Unit case (s) and e2e case (e) point `project_root` at the actual repo root and read REAL in-flight task folders. Both tests "skip if the directory doesn't exist (defensive — fresh-clone friendly)." But the directories EXIST in the current working tree — including their stage subfolders that may CHANGE during the lifetime of stage-3's branch. Specifically: if `.workflow_artifacts/artifact-format-architecture/stage-5/` (which exists today) gets cleaned up via `/end_of_task` archive between authoring and CI run, the test's silent skip masks a real regression.
  - Where: unit case (s); e2e case (e).
  - Why it matters: A test that "passes" because it silently skipped is exactly the lesson 2026-04-24 fixture-detection trap. Per architecture R-NINE ("The four in-flight task folders have unforeseen layout variations not covered by the five fixture subdirs … fails LOUDLY") — the loud failure is the whole POINT of these tests. Defensive skip undermines the canary value.
  - Suggestion: Replace the silent-skip with one of: (a) hard-assert each of the four task directories exists and the test fails with a clear "expected in-flight task folder absent — was it finalized? if so, REMOVE this test case" error message; (b) snapshot the layouts at e2e authoring time into a `.workflow_artifacts/quoin-foundation/stage-3/inflight-snapshot.txt` file (one line per folder: `<name> | <arch-md-status> | <current-plan-md-status> | <stage-folders-list>`) and assert the live state matches. The snapshot also makes the I-FIVE invariant explicit and reviewable. Per lesson 2026-04-22 (fixture-first traps when baselines rot): a snapshot is a stable contract; the live filesystem is not.

- **Issue M3 — Residual-hardcode grep procedure has gaps and counts CLAUDE.md hits as "expected" without verifying which lines**
  - What: The grep procedure says "CLAUDE.md may legitimately contain `<task-name>/current-plan.md` inside the new `### Multi-stage tasks` example diagram (the tree-format example showing `├── current-plan.md`) — that's a doc reference, not a runtime path." But the grep doesn't actually distinguish — it just notes the doc references are "expected" and excludes CLAUDE.md from the matches-flagged set. After the CLAUDE.md edit, the multi-stage section has multiple plausibly-matching lines (the tree-form example, the resolution-order rules block). Future edits to CLAUDE.md could ADD new accidentally-hardcoded `<task-name>/current-plan` strings outside the multi-stage section, and the grep would miss them. The procedure is currently "ZERO matches in skills/; CLAUDE.md may legitimately contain..." — the "may legitimately" is a verbal escape hatch, not an automated check.
  - Where: residual-hardcode grep procedure.
  - Why it matters: This grep is described as the "belt-and-suspenders" mitigation for R-THREE (the dominant risk for this stage). If its check is permissive enough to miss future regressions, it's not actually mitigating. Lesson 2026-04-24 (fixture filename auto-detection — "a test that passes because it never exercised the code path is worse than a failing test") applies.
  - Suggestion: Tighten grep (a) into two greps: (a1) `grep -rn '<task-name>/current-plan' dev-workflow/skills/` — expect EXACTLY 0; (a2) `grep -n '<task-name>/current-plan' dev-workflow/CLAUDE.md` — expect EXACTLY a documented small number (e.g., 2: one in the tree diagram, one in the grandfathering example) AND assert each match's line number is BETWEEN the `### Multi-stage tasks` heading and the next H2/H3 below it. The bounded-line-range check makes the doc carve-out explicit and machine-verifiable. Same hardening applies to greps (b) and (c).

- **Issue M4 — Q-ONE (architect-critic-N.md routing) is deferred but the resolver's silent rule-2 path may break it**
  - What: The plan's open question Q-ONE ("Does the resolver also route `architecture-critic-N.md` into stage subfolders, or always task root?") is deferred to S-FOUR. But the architect critic phase will invoke `/critic --target=architecture.md` from a stage subagent context. Current `critic/SKILL.md:122` says architecture-critic write target is `{project-folder}/.workflow_artifacts/{task-name}/architecture-critic-{round}.md` — task root. Per the plan's D-THREE, architecture.md ALWAYS lives at task root, and architecture-critic-N.md SHOULD too. But the SKILL-edit task row 3 (critic/SKILL.md edits) puts a generic resolver-prose template at line 122 that names "critic-response-N.md, review-N.md, gate-*.md: all under TASK-DIR" — and architecture-critic-N.md is NEITHER explicitly task-root NOR explicitly under TASK-DIR. The template is silent on it.
  - Where: critic/SKILL.md line 122; per-file edit template; D-THREE.
  - Why it matters: When S-FOUR (stage 4 of quoin-foundation) ships, the architect critic will read this prose. If the prose ambiguously points architecture-critic-N.md into the stage subfolder, S-FOUR's plan has to revisit S-THREE's edits in this stage. That's S-THREE leaking into S-FOUR. Per architecture I-FIVE + R-EIGHT: "Stage-4 inadvertently writes architecture-critic-N.md into a stage subfolder instead of task root."
  - Suggestion: Add an explicit line to the per-file edit template: "architecture-critic-N.md: ALWAYS `TASKROOT/architecture-critic-N.md` (parent-level, alongside architecture.md, NEVER under stage-N/). This is the D-THREE corollary for the architect critic phase." Update the e2e case (c) regex to add `architecture-critic` to the residual-hardcode sweep with an explicit task-root carve-out (alongside architecture.md and cost-ledger.md). This pre-resolves Q-ONE from S-FOUR's perspective without locking S-FOUR's design.

- **Issue M5 — Path-resolver test fixtures need Tier 1 carve-out documentation in CLAUDE.md**
  - What: The fixture-author task creates 5 subdirectories under `dev-workflow/scripts/tests/fixtures/path_resolve/` containing `architecture.md` stubs and empty `current-plan.md` files. These stubs are HAND-EDITED test fixtures (Tier 1 by the CLAUDE.md "any new file class" decision rule). But the fixture-author task doesn't add them to the Tier 1 carve-out section in CLAUDE.md (the existing carve-out lists `dev-workflow/scripts/tests/fixtures/quoin-stage-1-preamble.md` and `dev-workflow/scripts/verify_subagent_dispatch.md` — both very specific entries).
  - Where: fixture-author task acceptance criteria; CLAUDE.md Tier 1 source-files block.
  - Why it matters: A future caveman-token-optimization-like compression pass (or a future Tier-3 enforcement check) could look at these test fixtures and try to terse-compress them, breaking the resolver-unit-test deterministic assertions. The CLAUDE.md "If you are adding a new file class and unsure which tier applies: hand-edited or contract-approved → Tier 1" rule says the fixture-author task should add the carve-out.
  - Suggestion: Add a new acceptance bullet to the fixture-author task (or a small sub-task to the CLAUDE.md edit task): append to CLAUDE.md Tier 1 "Source files" block: `- dev-workflow/scripts/tests/fixtures/path_resolve/**` (Stage 3 path-resolver test fixture corpus; hand-edited Tier 1; consumed by `test_path_resolve.py`). Verify install.sh re-write logic preserves the addition.

- **Issue M6 — D-FOUR stage-name substring ambiguity has no automated detection of multi-match risk**
  - What: D-FOUR says "a name that's an accidental substring of two stage descriptions … would silently match the first." The mitigation is "user disambiguates via integer form." But the resolver doesn't WARN — it returns first-match silently. Combined with R-THREE ("Stage-name lookup ambiguity silently routes to wrong stage"), the user has no signal that a substring match was ambiguous.
  - Where: D-FOUR; R-THREE.
  - Why it matters: A future architecture decomposition with stage names like "data-migration" and "data-cleanup" — the user types "data" expecting an error — gets stage-N silently. Lesson 2026-04-24 (fixture detection silent false-pass) applies at the user-experience layer: the user can't distinguish a successful disambiguation from a silent first-match.
  - Suggestion: Modify `_lookup_stage_by_name` to collect ALL matches; if `len(matches) > 1`, raise `ValueError(f"path_resolve: stage name '{name}' matches {len(matches)} stages: ... — disambiguate by using --stage <integer>")`. This adds one unit case (`test_substring_multimatch_raises`) and one R-THREE mitigation update. The plan's claim "deterministic, debuggable" becomes actually-debuggable.

### Minor (improvement, use judgment)

- **Issue m1 — Smoke task cost discipline references a $0.50–$2 cap but cleanup/abort guidance is verbal-only**
  - Suggestion: Add to the smoke procedure step (f): "abort criteria: (i) orchestrator starts /plan or /critic phase (look for `Round 1 critic begins...` or similar in stdout); (ii) elapsed time exceeds 90 seconds; (iii) any cost line > $1.00 reported by the harness banner."

- **Issue m2 — Naming convention for the live-repo unit case differs slightly from stage-1's pattern**
  - Suggestion: Rename `test_real_repo_grandfathering_smoke` to `test_inflight_task_grandfathering_real_repo`. Cosmetic only — matches stage-1's `test_<area>_<assertion>` form.

- **Issue m3 — install.sh edit anchors (line 125 and 201) may shift if S-TWO (ccusage fallback) merges first**
  - Suggestion: Replace line-number anchors in the install.sh-edit task with line-content anchors: `for script_file in summarize_for_human.py validate_artifact.py; do` (and the success-message string-content anchor). This is the same pattern used by the SKILL-edit task's cell-content anchors. Robust to ordering of S-TWO / S-THREE merges.

## What's good

- Fixture-first ordering is correctly applied (the fixture-author task before the unit-test task before the resolver-author task; lesson 2026-04-22 fixture-first trap pre-emptively avoided by the "pure structural fixtures: empty files + minimal architecture.md stubs; no LLM-generated content" rationale).
- The 19 unit cases cover the resolver's type-dispatch matrix exhaustively; the case naming is precise and mostly conventional.
- D-02 explicitly documents the OPT-IN migration rule with a forward-pointer to S-FOUR — this is the right call given the in-flight task constraints.
- Risks R-ONE through R-TEN each map to a specific test or grep — the rigor matches stage-1's risk register.
- The smoke task cost cap range is appropriately bounded and references lesson 2026-04-22 by ID — good lineage.
- D-06 lineage to lesson 2026-04-23 is correct: structural tests carry the load; the live LLM smoke is the irreducible last 1%.
- The unit case (o) `ast.parse` import-check is a sharp regression guard against accidentally importing PyYAML or anthropic in `path_resolve.py`. Borrowed from stage-1's stdlib-only pattern.
- Loop-detection note: of the 6 MAJOR + 3 CRITICAL issues, none look "systemic." C1 / C3 are mechanical (anchor list / line numbers) and a single revision pass should clear them. C2 is a fixture redesign — also a single pass. M1 / M4 are template-language additions. M2 / M3 are tighten-the-asserts work. M5 is a one-line CLAUDE.md addition. M6 is a small resolver behavior change + one new unit case. The plan has good bones; round 2 should converge.

## Scorecard

| Criterion | Score | Notes |
|-----------|-------|-------|
| Completeness | fair | C1 missing 2 skills (run, architect); C2 fixture-vs-reality gap. |
| Correctness | fair | Anchor lines off (plan/SKILL.md, critic/SKILL.md:113, gate/SKILL.md:116); resolver semantics correct but error-path under-specified. |
| Integration safety | good | D-THREE well-anchored; grandfathering rules well-defined; only Q-ONE deferred. |
| Risk coverage | good | R-ONE..R-TEN each mapped to a test or grep; M6 surfaces an undocumented R-THREE sub-case. |
| Testability | fair | Case (s) silent-skip is a hidden risk; substring-multi-match silent first-match is a UX hole. |
| Implementability | fair | Anchor-line edits + missing skills mean implementer needs more verification work. |
| De-risking | good | Smoke task bounded; unit + e2e tests deterministic; recursion guard echoes stage-1 pattern. |
